issue_title: Fix AuthenticatedClientSessionEntity protostream encoding
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

Fields `redirectUri` and `action` may be `null` and need attention when unmarshalling. Similar to https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/30485

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

If a Keycloak node sends an `AuthenticatedClientSessionEntity` with any of those fields `null`, it should be `null` at the receiver side. 

### Actual behavior

It is set as an empty string as specified by protocol buffers, which does not allow `null` primitives. 

### How to Reproduce?

Caught by a test during the development of the new feature: https://github.com/pruivo/keycloak/blob/cfcb73cc78637516d760280dc75a7fc0d3a680b2/testsuite/model/src/test/java/org/keycloak/testsuite/model/loginfailure/RemoteLoginFailureTest.java#L150-L175



### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix AuthenticatedClientSessionEntity protostream encoding

For String fields that may be null, convert an empty string to null when
reading from Protostream

Fixes #30511

Signed-off-by: Pedro Ruivo <pruivo@redhat.com>

issue_title: "Client Offline Session Max" no longer available
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When editing the settings on the advanced tab of a client in the kc admin console, the option to set "Client Offline Session Max" is no longer available.

This is related to [20792](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/20792).
That issue stated, that the Max-option should not be available in clients if it is deactived in the realm (which makes sense).

The issue is closed and attached to PR [27493](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/27493).
That PR is called "Show expiration only when explicitly requested" (which makes sense also).

But if you look at the changes in the PR, you can see that the Max-option is removed from the advanced client settings entirely, regardless of the realm settings.

According to the [Server Administration Guide for version 25.0.0](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#con-advanced-settings_server_administration_guide), the "Client Offline Session Max" option should still be available

### Version

25.0.0

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

"Client Offline Session Max" should be an available client setting as long as "Offline Session Max Limited" is active in the realm settings.

### Actual behavior

"Client Offline Session Max" is never an available client setting regardless of the realm settings.

### How to Reproduce?

Activate "Offline Session Max Limited" in the realm settings and check if you can configure "Client Offline Session Max" in the client settings.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: [UI] group selection does not update attribute tab
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

Given that the "Attributes"-tab is selected in the "Groups" section, the attributes are not updated when changing the group.

### Version

25.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The attributes are refreshed each time a different group is selected in the tree-view.

### Actual behavior

The attributes remain the same for different groups selected if the Attributes-tab has already been opened.

### How to Reproduce?

1) Create two groups: "Parent" and "Child", while the latter is a child group of the former.
2) In the tree-view to the left, select the "Parent" group
3) Select the "Attributes" tab on the right
4) Create an arbitrary attribute (key-value pair)
5) Expand the tree-view to the left and select the "Child" group
6) Notice that the attribute which was created for the "Parent" group is still being displayed
7) Switch to "Role mapping" or any other tab on the right and then switch back to the tab "Attributes"
8) Notice that the attribute has now vanished

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Localization prevents update of user-profile attributes
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

I am logged into the Keycloak admin console as the master realm admin.

When I now open my custom realm, go to "realm settings", switch to the tab "user profil", open e.g. the "username" attribute, change e.g. the validators and safe, i get the following error:
"Error! User Profile configuration has not been saved Please add translations before saving."

Whats causing the error is that in my custom realm (not the master realm, into which I am logged in, but the realm i want to change settings for) I have localization enabled and the supported locale as well as the default locale set to "german" only.

### Version

25.0.0

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

User attributes should be updateable regardless of the localization settings of the affected realm.

### Actual behavior

The update of user attributes results in an error, if localization is enabled and the supported locale as well as the default locale are set to certain languages.

### How to Reproduce?

Log into the Keycloak admin console as master realm admin.
Create a new custom realm.
For the new realm enable the localization and set the supported locale as well as the default locale to "german" only.
Go to the user profile settings and open the "username" attribute.
Add a random additional validator to the "username" attribute.
Try to save.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: client-jwt authentication fails on Token Introspection Endpoint
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

A client whose authentication method is `client-jwt` cannot access Keycloak's Token Introspection Endpoint.

### Version

25.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Token Introspeciton endopont returns a normal response.

### Actual behavior

Token Introspeciton endpoint returns the follwoing error:

```
2024-06-20 11:31:18 2024-06-20 02:31:18,406 ERROR [org.keycloak.services] (executor-thread-18) KC-SERVICES0025: Error when validating client assertion: java.lang.RuntimeException: Token audience doesn't match domain. Expected audiences are any of [https://as.keycloak-fapi.org/auth/realms/test, https://as.keycloak-fapi.org/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/token, https://as.keycloak-fapi.org/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/ext/par/request, https://as.keycloak-fapi.org/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/ext/ciba/auth] but audience from token is '[https://as.keycloak-fapi.org/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/token/introspect]'
2024-06-20 11:31:18     at org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.client.JWTClientAuthenticator.authenticateClient(JWTClientAuthenticator.java:108)
...
```

### How to Reproduce?

1. prepare a client  whose authentication method is `client-jwt`.
2. the client receives an access token from Keycloak.
3. the client send a request to Token Introspeciton endpoint with parameters like client_assertion_type=urn%3Aietf%3Aparams%3Aoauth%3Aclient-assertion-type%3Ajwt-bearer&client_assertion=...

### Anything else?

The body of client_assertion is as follows:
```
{
  "exp": 1718850519,
  "iat": 1718850459,
  "iss": "resource-server",
  "jti": "8fde33a23066cdaa42ac07d28574aa50",
  "aud": "https://as.keycloak-fapi.org/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/token/introspect",
  "sub": "resource-server"
}
```
Keycloak check its audience claim and returns an error because Keycloak recognized the follwoing url as valid values:
```
https://as.keycloak-fapi.org/auth/realms/test
https://as.keycloak-fapi.org/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/token,
https://as.keycloak-fapi.org/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/ext/par/request,
https://as.keycloak-fapi.org/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/ext/ciba/auth
```
It should be as follows:
```
https://as.keycloak-fapi.org/auth/realms/test
https://as.keycloak-fapi.org/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/token,
https://as.keycloak-fapi.org/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/token/introspect,
https://as.keycloak-fapi.org/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/ext/par/request,
https://as.keycloak-fapi.org/auth/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/ext/ciba/auth
```

client-jwt authentication fails on Token Introspection Endpoint

closes #30599

issue_title: token exchange: exchange-sequence fails with Client session for client 'client-exchanger' not present in user session
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

token-exchange

### Describe the bug

when i try to do a token-exchange in sequence it fails on the second exchange:
`[org.keycloak.services.managers.AuthenticationManager] Client session for client '..' not present in user session '..'`

still not shure if it's a plain "bug" or if i just miss something. i created https://stackoverflow.com/questions/78602573/keycloak-token-exchange-sequence with no input so far. 

there is a discussion to from https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/17440#discussioncomment-9760955 from @ChrisBrandhorst which seems to hit the same issue



### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

token exchange sequence should work for as many clients/audiences you like

### Actual behavior

the second exchange fails when initial token was received by `client_credentials` grant od public-client

### How to Reproduce?

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/30615
`org.keycloak.testsuite.oauth.ClientTokenExchangeTest`
- `testExchangeSequenceStartedWithGrantTypePassword` works
- `testExchangeSequenceStartedWithGrantTypeClientCredentials` fails
- `testExchangeSequenceStartedWithGrantTypePasswordOnPublicClient` fails

https://github.com/flyinfish/keycloak-examples/blob/main/token-exchange-sequence/README.md

_No response_

issue_title: Make sure not possible to import jakarta classes in admin-client-jee
issue_body:
### Description

To prevent issues like #30308, it can be good to make sure that it is not possible to import `jakarta.*` classes inside the `keycloak-admin-client-jee`. This is currently possible because of the dependency on `org.jboss.resteasy:resteasy-client-api:6.7.7.Final` in the `keycloak-admin-client-jee`, which should not be here as `keycloak-admin-client-jee` should have dependency on older resteasy version `4.7.7.Final`.

This is what inside `mvn dependency:tree` executed inside the `integration/admin-client-jee` directory:

```
...
[INFO] +- org.jboss.resteasy:resteasy-client:jar:4.7.7.Final:compile
[INFO] |  +- org.jboss.resteasy:resteasy-client-api:jar:6.2.7.Final:compile
[INFO] |  |  \- jakarta.ws.rs:jakarta.ws.rs-api:jar:3.1.0:compile
...
```



### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

_No response_

### Details

_No response_

Make sure not possible to import jakarta classes in admin-client-jee

commit_message:
Use regular CREATE TABLE instead of CREATE TEMPORARY

Some hosted/managed environments like google CloudSQL, might not support this type of statement (i.e. when using replication and GTID)

Since we are dropping the table anyway it seems a regular CREATE statement should work fine here.

Signed-off-by: mike-pt <mike-pt@users.noreply.github.com>

issue_title: LDAP connection pool params(maxsize, initsize, prefsize) picked up from backend ComponentModel and are not visible in Keycloak admin console
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

Setting JVM arguments to configure com.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool.maxsize, com.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool.initsize, com.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool.prefsize  is not taking effect.

It seems if parameters like connectionPoolingMaxSize are [set](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/LDAPIdentityStoreRegistry.java#L77-L79) in Keycloak realm(by importing previous version realm export, or migrating from previous version to current version) then the system property does not take effect. 

Now that the connectionPooling parameters for maxsize, initsize, prefsize are [removed](https://docs.google.com/document/d/18qhcpqxooJS9Q2pOZpsvl6qjuLiddX5gGUtqx3R6k8E/edit#heading=h.xg0uqakk01nr) from Keycloak admin console - there should be an option to configure these parameters or allow the system property configurations to take effect.

### Version

25.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Setting JVM arguments to configure following ldap connection pool parameters - com.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool.maxsize, com.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool.initsize, com.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool.prefsize - should take effect.

### Actual behavior

Setting JVM arguments to configure following ldap connection pool parameters - com.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool.maxsize, com.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool.initsize, com.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool.prefsize - do not take effect.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Start Keycloak server
2. Create a new realm and add a ldap user federation
3. Create a realm export of this newly created realm
4. Add connectionPoolingMaxSize in the realm export 
~~~
 "org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageProvider": [
      {
        "id": "ec8ff950-0ffa-4fd2-83dc-69827015a0c6",
        "name": "ldap",
        "providerId": "ldap",
......
        "config": {
          "connectionPoolingMaxSize": [
            "50"
          ],
          "fullSyncPeriod": [
            "-1"
          ],
~~~

5. Set  `JAVA_OPTS_APPEND="-Dcom.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool.debug=all -Dcom.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool.initsize=4 -Dcom.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool.maxsize=100 -Dcom.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool.prefsize=10"` 
6. Restart the Keycloak server with above JAVA_OPTS_APPEND set
7. Try to execute  a LDAP operation like Sync all users  - and check for following section in the logs:
~~~
***** start *****
idle timeout: 300000
maximum pool size: 50
preferred pool size: 10
initial pool size: 4
protocol types: plain ssl
authentication types: none simple 
~~~
8. The JVM argument -Dcom.sun.jndi.ldap.connect.pool.maxsize=100 was ineffective and max size was set to connectionPoolingMaxSize.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
This fix allows the LDAP connection pool parameters - maxsize, prefsize, initsize - to be configured using JVM arguments.

Removed the check on connectionPoolingMaxSize, connectionPoolingInitSize and connectionPoolingPrefSize

Closes #30677

Signed-off-by: Rishabh Singh <rishabhsvats@gmail.com>

This fix allows all the LDAP connection pool parameters to be configured using JVM arguments.

Removed all the ldap connection pool parameters

Signed-off-by: Rishabh Singh <rishabhsvats@gmail.com>

issue_title: Require SSL mode "External requests" does not work with IPv6 local addresses
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When using IPv6 and client is sending request from link-local or unique local address (ULA), client is blocked from sending clear-text HTTP requests when realm configuration is set to require SSL for "External request" ([link](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_ssl_modes)).



### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Requests sent from IPv6 link-local or unique local address should succeed.

### Actual behavior

Requests are rejected with response `403 Forbidden` with body

```json
{
    "error": "invalid_request",
    "error_description": "HTTPS required"
}
```


### How to Reproduce?

One way to reproduce is to use Kubernetes cluster with IPv6 enabled

1. Run Keycloak in a Kubernetes cluster with IPv6 enabled.
2. Run a client in another pod and check that the pod's network interface has IPv6 address either from link-local address range or unique local address range. Send request using IPv6.
3. Observe response `403 Forbidden`

For example for local development, Kind would use link-local addresses for pods and services within the cluster and a "real" CNI like Calico would typically use unique local addresses (ULA).

### Anything else?

The code that checks for the request is here https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/ece72cd491b68422940136bc6171392e07db6679/common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/common/enums/SslRequired.java#L53-L54

Looking at the git history, it seems that initially the code aimed to cover only clients within localhost, and later it took the current form where it was extended to allow IPv4 private addresses ([link](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/6cbeeb1b1a772510b00881ea5ed5ea11379115ca)). 

In case of IPv6 the private address ranges are following:

|address type|binary prefix|IPv6 notation|
|-|-|-|
| Link-local unicast | 1111 1110 10 | FE80::/10 |
| Unique local (ULA) | 1111 110 | FC00::/7 |
| Site-local unicast (deprecated) | 1111 1110 11 | FEF0::/10 |

While the JDK method `Inet4Address.isSiteLocalAddress()` covers private address ranges for IPv4 ([link](https://github.com/openjdk/jdk17u/blob/c9d83d392f3809bf536afdcb52142ee6916acff0/src/java.base/share/classes/java/net/Inet4Address.java#L210-L221)), the IPv6 version `Inet6Address.isSiteLocalAddress()` only covers site-local unicast address range ([link](https://github.com/openjdk/jdk17u/blob/c9d83d392f3809bf536afdcb52142ee6916acff0/src/java.base/share/classes/java/net/Inet6Address.java#L332-L335)). According to [RFC4291 section2.5.7](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc4291#section-2.5.7) this address range is now deprecated and should be considered as global address by new implementation

> The special behavior of this prefix defined in [RFC3513] must no longer be supported in new implementations (i.e., new implementations must treat this prefix as Global Unicast).
>
> Existing implementations and deployments may continue to use this prefix.

While the documentation for interface `InetAddress.isSiteLocalAddress()` says ([link](https://github.com/openjdk/jdk17u/blob/c9d83d392f3809bf536afdcb52142ee6916acff0/src/java.base/share/classes/java/net/InetAddress.java#L413C1-L422C6))

>   Utility routine to check if the InetAddress is a site local address.

it seems likely to me that applications may rather use it for the effect of "all private address ranges", than for the specific (now deprecated) IPv6 site-local definition, especially considering the method is used through the upper class that covers both IPv4 and IPv6. Still, the JDK IPv6 implementation seems to take strict interpretation of covering site-local only and does not offer any method for checking for IPv6 private address ranges.
commit_message:
Allow requests from local IPv6 addresses

If administrator selects EXTERNAL for Require SSL setting, allow clear-text
HTTP requests when client is coming from IPv6 link-local or unique local
address (ULA).

Previously only private IPv4 addresses were allowed and private IPv6 addresses
were rejected.

Closes #30678

Signed-off-by: Tero Saarni <tero.saarni@est.tech>

issue_title: Full details of errors not shown in admin  and account console
issue_body:
When an error returned by they REST API is shown to the user in the admin or account console not all details are shown. For example, when adding an execution to a built-in flow the following message is displayed:

![An error message stating: "Could not update flow: For more on this error consult the server log at the debug level."](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/695720/b17c602b-5aef-451c-82b8-5799596694bf)

However, looking at the error response from the API much more detail is present that is relevant to the user:

```json
{
  "error": "It is illegal to add execution to a built in flow",
  "error_description": "For more on this error consult the server log at the debug level."
}
```

These details should be shown in the alert as well, so that both the `error` and `error_description` fields are presented to the user.

issue_title: Protobuf deserialization has a default of an empty String
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Acceptance criteria

* Adding toggling the new behavior in protostream library
* Protostream release with the new feature
* Adapting the Keycloak code base to use the toggle
* Consider a soon-ish productized version of protostream (make it part of this issue, or create a new issue for KC26)

### Describe the bug

In protocol buffers v3 the default value of a String is an empty string. Due to that in the current implementation, all properties of a serialized objected with are null strings will become an empty string.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

A null String field should still be null after serialization and de-serialization. 

### Actual behavior

The value will become an empty String instead. 

In commits 66dd9e65b97b06321b3d07856e1bc56c267d1184 and 67098f04694cf0e06a4bd25c477b5110313a7228, this has already been fixed by treating any empty string as a null value. 

We are still missing it in other places like for example the brokerSessionId in the UserSessionEntity. 

### Anything else?

In a team discussion on Jun 25 I raised my concern that the test suite of Keycloak is not prepared to find those test failures as they will appear only when using the new external Infinispan setup where values are always serialized, or in a setup with multiple Keycloak nodes where keys are read from and written to another node. 

To keep the existing behavior of `MarshallUtil.marshallString` which kept null values, we were discussed with @pruivo, @ryanemerson, @mhajas and @kami619 to ask for a similar behavior in protostream. Ideally we would like to have a central switch to have all String values treated as null by default. 

Once the change is done, `Marshalling.emptyStringToNull` should no longer be necessary and can therefore be removed.
commit_message:
Allow null Protostream fields

Closes #30761

Co-authored-by: Pedro Ruivo <pruivo@redhat.com>
Signed-off-by: Ryan Emerson <remerson@redhat.com>

issue_title: Check for being Offline type in refresh token flow must be done based on refresh token request parameter
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

Based on [Refreshing an Access Token](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-6) : 

> The authorization server MAY issue a new refresh token, in which case
   the client MUST discard the old refresh token and replace it with the
   new refresh token.  The authorization server MAY revoke the old
   refresh token after issuing a new refresh token to the client. 

In Keycloak you can not return refresh token in refresh token flow using `SuppressRefreshTokenRotationExecutor` policy executor.
Another way is after geting refresh token to disable 'Use refresh tokens' client parameter.

User get refresh token for a client  'Use refresh tokens' including `offline_access` scope. This is offline type refresh token.
User do refresh token flow. A new access token will be returned without a new refresh token.

[This PR](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/12196) has added check for being offline type based on return refresh token (if exists).
As you see from above scenario, this could lead to an offline refresh flow to characterize as no offline.
This lead in [RefreshTokenGrantType](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/grants/RefreshTokenGrantType.java) line 85 to try update user session for offline user session.

For regular cases, although no error is thrown, I do not know what happens during offline user session update.
**The most important is that this scenario will not work during Keycloak restart (or upgrade). After restart, if you try to execute refresh token flow with a refresh token produced before restart, a NullPointerException will be thrown .As you know, offline refresh token lives after restart**

**Check for refresh token must be done based on refresh token request parameter.**

### Version

25.0.1

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

- In the scenario mentioned, user session must not refreshed in refresh token flow.

- Offline refresh token flow must always work with offline refresh token produced before Keycloak restart.

### Actual behavior

- In the scenario mentioned, user session is refreshed in refresh token flow.

- Offline refresh token flow with offline refresh token produced before Keycloak restart, returns NullPointerException.

### How to Reproduce?

- Client with 'Use refresh tokens' parameter.
- Use `SuppressRefreshTokenRotationExecutor` policy executor for this client.
- User get refresh token for this client  including `offline_access` scope. This is offline type refresh token.
- Keycloak restart
- Execute refresh token flow with a refresh token produced before restart


### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Check refresh token flow response for offline based on refresh token request parameter

Closes #30857

Signed-off-by: cgeorgilakis-grnet <cgeorgilakis@admin.grnet.gr>

issue_title: Incorrect version comparison in ModelVersion
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

A [typo](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/migration/ModelVersion.java#L97) in `ModelVersion` results in incorrect version comparison if a qualifier (the fourth component) is used, for example, in a custom Keycloak build:

```java
var foo = new ModelVersion("24.0.4.0");
var bar = new ModelVersion("24.0.0");
foo.lessThan(bar); // true
bar.lessThan(foo); // also true
```

This could result in unwanted migration executions, e.g. `MigrateTo24_0_0` will be run even if the current model version is 24.0.4.0. As a side effect, this will cause failures in `ExportImportTest` (because it checks if the imported realm is identical to the exported one, and this will not be the case because of erroneous migration).

### Version

25.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Micro version and qualifier should be handled properly in version comparison, and no erroneous migrations should occur.

### Actual behavior

When using custom versioning scheme with a qualifier, the comparison is not performed correctly, there are erroneous migrations and test failures in `ExportImportTest`.

### How to Reproduce?

* set Keycloak version to 24.0.4.0 and build
* run `ExportImportTest`

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Incorrect version comparison in ModelVersion

Closes #30935

issue_title: Vulnerability in brute force detection settings
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

The following is a suspected vulnerability in the brute force detection settings in Keycloak version **24.0.1**

Setting the brute force mode to “lockout temporarily,” and then setting the “wait increment” and “max wait” to too high of a value breaks the locking out functionality.

If you configure brute force detection with the following settings, the locking out of an account will not work.

Brute force mode:    Lockout temporarily
Max login failures:       3
Wait increment:           21900 days
Max wait:             21900 days
Failure reset time:    15 minutes

Quick login check milliseconds:     1000
Minimum quick login wait:          1 minute

After 3 failed attempts of logging in, the user should be locked out, though by using the above settings prevents the locking out of the account.

I suspect this may be an integer overflow issue. If days are converted to seconds, at 21900 days, the total seconds approaches the 32 bit signed integer max.

### Version

24.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

For the frontend to not allow for such a high value to be entered into the “wait increment” and “max wait”.
For the backend to protect from such a high value to be set.

### Actual behavior

If you configure brute force detection with the following settings, the locking out of an account will not work.

Brute force mode:    Lockout temporarily
Max login failures:       3
Wait increment:           21900 days
Max wait:             21900 days
Failure reset time:    15 minutes

Quick login check milliseconds:     1000
Minimum quick login wait:          1 minute

After 3 failed attempts of logging in, the user should be locked out, though by using the above settings prevents the locking out of the account.

### How to Reproduce?

If you configure brute force detection with the following settings, the locking out of an account will not work.

Brute force mode:    Lockout temporarily
Max login failures:       3
Wait increment:           21900 days
Max wait:             21900 days

Quick login check milliseconds:     1000

issue_title: Brute force protection: Lockout permanently uses parameters configured under lockout temporarily
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

Configure Brute force protection in a Realm:

1. Choose mixed mode and set wait increment to 0
2. save
3. Choose lockout permanently mode and set e.g. max login failes 10, quick login check millis 0, minimum quick login wait empty
4. save
Result: realm.getMaxTemporaryLockouts() == 0 and realm.getWaitIncrementSeconds() == 0

Now login with wrong password 10 times and observe that the user is NOT disabled.

### Version

25.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

User gets locked

### Actual behavior

User does not get locked

### How to Reproduce?

see description

### Anything else?

DefaultBruteForceProtector (hat commit 6a22f31b812ce68735373b1a0b047b75958f0b64 = tag 25.0.1)
- line 119: user gets locked
- line 113: therefore user must have numTemporaryLockouts (=0) > realm.getMaxTemporaryLockouts() (=0)
- line 99: would have incremented numTemporaryLockouts but only if waitSeconds > 0

Why are parameters of temporary lockout relevant for permanent lockout? You can't even configure those when choosing permanent lockout mode.

Workaround: use reproduction steps but set wait increment to at least 1 sec.
commit_message:
Brute force protection: Lockout permanently uses parameters configured under lockout temporarily
Closes #30969

issue_title: Realm cannot be deleted if there are tons of consents
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

When deleting a realm with a large number of USER_CONSENT_CLIENT_SCOPE records, following slow query logs are recorded and the realm deletion operation failed with transaction timeout error:

```
# Time: 2024-05-12T01:23:45.000000Z
# User@Host: root[root] @  [127.0.0.1]  Id: 13895
# Query_time: 31.325039  Lock_time: 0.000002 Rows_sent: 0  Rows_examined: 14200000
SET timestamp=1715499224;
delete from USER_CONSENT_CLIENT_SCOPE where SCOPE_ID='1bb77779-8416-5f39-38ec-c741a7955262'; 
```

This is because there is no index for that table in column scope_id:

```
create index IDX_USCONSENT_SCOPEID on USER_CONSENT_CLIENT_SCOPE(SCOPE_ID);
```

After that there is another slow query because of the insidious bug of mariadb/mysql of not using indexes with inner queries:

```
# Time: 2024-06-01T05:43:21.000000Z
# User@Host: root[root] @  [127.0.0.1]  Id:   101
# Query_time: 570.563298  Lock_time: 0.000002 Rows_sent: 0  Rows_examined: 2110000
